name: cc-skill-project-guidelines-example
capabilities: Provides complete architecture diagrams for Next.js and FastAPI applications
Documents TypeScript and Python code patterns for API development
Includes Claude AI integration examples with structured output
Contains testing workflows for pytest and React Testing Library
Offers deployment checklists and Google Cloud Run commands
Establishes critical coding rules like immutability and TDD requirements
author: affaan-m
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation-only resource containing project guidelines and code examples. All 47 static analyzer findings are false positives resulting from Markdown code fences being misinterpreted as shell commands. The file contains no executable code, no actual network calls, and no credential access - only documentation of best practices for a full-stack application.
valueStatement: Developers struggle with maintaining consistent patterns across full-stack projects. This skill provides ready-to-use architecture diagrams, code patterns, and deployment workflows for Next.js and FastAPI applications with Claude AI integration.
userTitle: Build Full-Stack AI Applications with Project Guidelines
limitations: This skill provides documentation only - it does not execute code or automate tasks
Examples are specific to a Zenith-style AI platform architecture
Requires manual adaptation for projects with different tech stacks
Environment variable examples must be replaced with actual credentials

useCases.title: Onboard New Team Members
useCases.description: Use this skill to quickly bring developers up to speed on project architecture, code patterns, and deployment workflows.

useCases.title: Standardize Code Reviews
useCases.description: Reference the documented patterns and critical rules during code reviews to ensure consistency across the codebase.

useCases.title: Setup New Project Instances
useCases.description: Clone the documented structure and patterns when creating new projects with similar full-stack architecture.

promptTemplates.title: Review Project Architecture
promptTemplates.prompt: Based on the project guidelines, explain the architecture for our Next.js and FastAPI application. Include the service diagram and how Claude AI integrates with the backend.

promptTemplates.title: Generate API Response Pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Using the documented API response format from the guidelines, create a Pydantic model for a user profile endpoint that returns success, data, and error fields.

promptTemplates.title: Setup Test Structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Following the testing requirements in the guidelines, generate pytest test fixtures and a sample test for the health check endpoint in our FastAPI backend.

promptTemplates.title: Prepare Deployment Checklist
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the deployment workflow guidelines and generate a pre-deployment checklist for our next Cloud Run release. Include all required environment variables and build steps.

bestPractices: Write tests before implementation following TDD methodology with minimum 80% coverage
Maintain immutability - never mutate objects or arrays directly in TypeScript or Python code
Keep files small with 200-400 lines typical and 800 lines maximum for maintainability
antiPatterns: Using console.log in production code - use proper logging frameworks instead
Hardcoding secrets or credentials in source files - always use environment variables
Skipping input validation - always validate with Pydantic for Python or Zod for TypeScript

faq.question: What tech stack does this project use?
faq.answer: Frontend: Next.js 15 with App Router, TypeScript, and TailwindCSS. Backend: FastAPI with Python 3.11 and Pydantic. Database: Supabase PostgreSQL. AI: Claude API with tool calling. Deployment: Google Cloud Run.

faq.question: How do I run tests for this project?
faq.answer: Backend: use 'poetry run pytest tests/' with optional coverage flags. Frontend: use 'npm run test' for unit tests and 'npm run test:e2e' for Playwright end-to-end tests.

faq.question: What are the critical coding rules I must follow?
faq.answer: No emojis in code, maintain immutability, follow TDD with 80% coverage minimum, keep files under 800 lines, no console.log in production, use try/catch for error handling, and validate all inputs with Pydantic or Zod.

faq.question: How do I deploy this application?
faq.answer: Build the frontend with 'npm run build' then deploy with 'gcloud run deploy frontend --source'. Build and deploy the backend with 'gcloud run deploy backend --source'. Ensure all tests pass and no hardcoded secrets exist before deploying.

faq.question: What environment variables are required?
faq.answer: Frontend needs NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY. Backend needs DATABASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, SUPABASE_URL, and SUPABASE_KEY. Never commit these values to version control.

faq.question: How is Claude AI integrated into the backend?
faq.answer: The backend uses the Anthropic Python SDK with structured output via tool calling. Define Pydantic models for expected output schemas, then use client.messages.create with tool_choice to force structured responses that can be parsed directly.
